reasoning: The market was priced at 0.06 (6%) on October 16, 2025, with 15 days remaining until the October 31 resolution date. My searches up to the knowledge cutoff of October 26, 2025 (5 days remaining) show no reports of strikes, attacks, or notable breaking escalations between Israel and Iran that would either trigger the market or suggest an imminent breakdown of the ceasefire established in June 2025. While there were analytical reports in early October warning of potential strikes, these expected dates (like early October or October 7) have passed without incident. Given the lack of developments and the natural time decay over the past 10 days, the probability of a strike occurring in the final 5 days has roughly dropped by two-thirds from the initial October 16 baseline. Therefore, 2% reflects the baseline risk of sudden escalation in the remaining timeframe.
